title: Ithycaulon brasiliense
intro: Ithycaulon brasiliense, also known as "pata de cabra" or "goat's foot," is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Ithycaulon brasiliense include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ithycaulon brasiliense is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, often used to aid in the treatment of infections, pain, and inflammation.
